title: JIT performance benchmarks and adaptive compilation tuning
purpose: Validate that JIT compilation provides measurable speedup over interpretation and tune the compilation threshold. This brief adds benchmarks comparing interpreted vs compiled execution for representative workloads.
task: Create benchmarks, measure speedup, and tune the compilation threshold based on results.

requirements.title: Benchmark suite
requirements.spec: Create benchmarks comparing interpreted and JIT-compiled execution for representative functions.
acceptance: Benchmark: list processing (map, filter, fold over 10K elements)
Benchmark: pattern matching intensive (case with 20 clauses)
Benchmarks run with criterion crate for statistical rigor
Results document speedup factor for each workload

requirements.title: Adaptive compilation threshold
requirements.spec: Tune the compilation threshold based on benchmark results.
acceptance: Default threshold adjusted based on benchmark data
Threshold is configurable at runtime via VM config
Functions below threshold execute faster interpreted (compilation overhead > benefit)
Test: verify threshold prevents compilation of rarely-called functions

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement profile-guided optimization (PGO) — simple call counting only
SHALL NOT implement tiered compilation (multiple optimization levels) — single tier
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
